text: Extension must support direct API calls to sanctioned providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) without requiring a localhost proxy.
Vault data flow must use only git via corporate-approved remote. Extension must never require direct network access to h
Features depending on localhost services (RAG search, health status, embedding) must fail silently with informative UI,
API credentials resolved from VS Code settings, environment variables, or pass store (in that order). Per-machine settin
All hapax repositories must have .gitignore patterns for sensitive files (.env, *.pem, *.key, credentials.json). No trac
Adding a new LLM provider must require only implementing a provider interface and adding a settings entry, not structura
Core chat functionality (conversation, note context, slash commands) must work identically regardless of provider select
All agents must be runnable with zero configuration or setup steps beyond environment variables.
Agent entry points must not require the operator to remember command-line arguments or flags.
Error messages must include specific next actions, not just descriptions of what went wrong.
Agents must gracefully handle missing dependencies and provide clear installation instructions.
Agents must persist their own state and resume work without requiring the operator to remember where they left off.
State transitions and progress must be visible without the operator having to check logs or debug output.
Recurring tasks must be automated rather than requiring manual triggering by the operator.
Agents like health_monitor and drift_detector must self-schedule and report completion status.
Configuration files must use sensible defaults and avoid requiring the operator to make low-stakes decisions.
Interactive prompts must not ask questions the system can reasonably answer itself.
Long-running agents must provide regular progress updates without being queried.
Success states must be explicitly confirmed rather than indicated by absence of error messages.
Agents must maintain sufficient context to explain their current state and recent actions when queried.
Status outputs must include timestamps and be human-readable without additional parsing.
Critical alerts must be delivered through external channels (notifications, email) rather than requiring log monitoring.
Error messages must include specific next steps rather than generic failure descriptions.
System failures must provide automatic recovery or graceful degradation without operator diagnosis.
Configuration files must have comprehensive defaults that work in the operator's environment.
Agent dependencies must be automatically resolved or clearly documented with installation scripts.
Interactive prompts must timeout with sensible defaults rather than hanging indefinitely.
Multi-step workflows must checkpoint progress to allow resumption after interruption.
Documentation must focus on "what this does for you" rather than "how this works internally".
Log messages must distinguish between "needs attention" and "normal operation" without requiring operator to learn what'
Alert conditions must be actionable and include enough context to respond without additional investigation.
Batch operations must provide summary reports rather than requiring operator to synthesize results from logs.
System must persist task context across interruptions to eliminate restart cognitive overhead.
Alert mechanisms (notify.py, alert_state.py) must proactively surface actionable items rather than requiring operator to
Configuration files must provide sensible defaults that work without operator customization.
Error handling must auto-recover or auto-escalate rather than leaving operator in undefined states requiring diagnosis.
Routine maintenance agents (knowledge_maint, health_monitor) must run autonomously on schedules, not on-demand.
Agent outputs must include clear next-action recommendations to reduce decision paralysis.
Interfaces must minimize context switching by batching related information in single views (briefing.py, digest.py).
System must automatically capture and surface relevant historical context without requiring operator to recall or search
Long-running processes must be resumable and provide progress indicators to support task switching.
When multiple options exist, system must recommend the path of least cognitive resistance as the default.
Multi-round deliberations must pass at least 2 of 3 process-tracing hoop tests (position shift, argument tracing, counte
Multi-round deliberation activation rate (fraction of pre-committed update conditions acknowledged as met) must exceed 1
Concession asymmetry ratio across the rolling window of recent deliberations must not exceed 3.0x, indicating one agent
Deliberation activation rate trend must not show sustained decline across batches, which would indicate governance proce
Skill definitions (SKILL.md files) must be syntactically valid. YAML frontmatter must include name and description. Embe
Governance artifacts (precedents, audit entries) must have lifecycle management with escalation. Agent-created precedent
All LLM-generated text outputs — agent reports, briefings, documentation, status messages, demo scripts, and commit mess
The system must not store, persist, or accumulate state about a specific non-operator person (location, presence pattern
A consent contract must record explicit opt-in by the subject. Implied consent, assumed consent, or operator-only consen
Each consent contract must specify a visibility mechanism through which the subject can inspect all data the system hold
Either party (operator or subject) may revoke a consent contract at any time. Upon revocation, the system must purge all
Each consent contract must enumerate the specific data categories permitted (e.g., coarse_location, presence, biometrics
Transient environmental perception (e.g., VAD detecting a voice, a figure appearing on a camera feed) does not require a
Inferred or derived state about a non-operator person (e.g., predicted arrival time, habitual patterns) counts as persis
All consent contracts must be stored with creation timestamp, parties, scope, and revocation status. Contract history mu
Any PerceptionBackend or data ingestion pathway that receives data about non-operator persons must verify an active cons
Third-party content the system redistributes to any audience (stream overlays, omg.lol surfaces, CDN-published assets) m
Never generate feedback language, performance evaluations, or coaching recommendations directed at individual team membe
Never suggest what the operator should say to a team member or draft language for delivery in people conversations.
1:1 meeting cadence must be the primary staleness signal for people management, auto-detected from frontmatter date fiel
Cognitive load assessment must come from operator self-report in frontmatter only, never from LLM inference about a pers
Management state collection (people, meetings, coaching, feedback) must be fully deterministic with zero LLM calls.
Prep agents focus on signal aggregation and open-loop surfacing, not people recommendations or relationship advice.
Work domain data respects the work/home boundary via the bridge zone. No work-specific PII leaks into personal domain to
All authentication, authorization, and user management code must be removed or disabled since there is exactly one autho
Configuration files should hardcode the operator's preferences rather than providing generic defaults or multi-user opti
All data storage can assume single-user ownership without access control, permissions checks, or data isolation.
Privacy controls, data anonymization, and consent mechanisms for the operator's own data are unnecessary since the user
Error messages can include sensitive debugging information since only the operator will see them.
Logging can be more verbose and include personal details since logs are private to the operator.
User interfaces should optimize for the operator's specific workflows rather than general usability patterns.
Code comments and variable names can reference the operator's personal context without explanation.
Multi-tenant security measures, rate limiting per user, and user input validation for malicious intent are unnecessary.
Performance optimizations should target single-user load patterns rather than concurrent multi-user scenarios.
API endpoints can assume trusted caller and skip user verification, quotas, and abuse prevention.
File paths, database schemas, and data structures can use operator-specific identifiers without generalization.
All notifications, alerts, and communications can be addressed directly to the operator without user preference systems.
Deployment and infrastructure should optimize for single-instance usage rather than multi-tenant architecture.
Performance optimizations should prioritize single-user workflows over multi-user scalability concerns.
Audit logs and monitoring can omit user identification fields since all activity is attributed to the operator by defini
Deployment and infrastructure configuration must assume single-user access patterns and can skip multi-tenancy considera
Features for user collaboration, sharing between users, or multi-user coordination must not be developed.
All architectural and design decisions must be evaluated primarily for how they serve the operator's specific needs rath
File paths and database queries must not dynamically construct user-specific paths or apply user-based filters.
System architecture must not include user-based load balancing, user sharding, or per-user scaling mechanisms.
Caching strategies must not implement user-specific cache keys or user-scoped cache invalidation patterns.
Administrative interfaces, user management UIs, or role assignment systems must not exist since the single user is the a
Notification systems in shared/notify.py must not support user-specific notification preferences or multi-user delivery
AI agents must not implement user context switching, user permission checking, or user-specific behavior customization.
